$source: Manually curated. A future refresh script (scripts/refresh_pr_algorithms_constants.py) will update `rates` from the AWS Pricing API + salary surveys, and `heuristics` from in-house telemetry.

money.title: BLS — Software Developer Occupational Stats (US wages)
Sonar — Cost of technical debt (dev-hours/yr per 1M LOC)
Testomat — Real cost of software bugs (defect escalation)
Stanford Digital Economy Lab — how AI agents spend tokens

runtime.title: Google SRE Book — Monitoring distributed systems

runtime_ux.title: Google SRE Book — Being on-call (developer UX)

nfr.title: ISO/IEC 25010 — Software product quality model (canonical NFR taxonomy)

dev_hour_usd.notes: US fully-loaded mid-senior engineer rate. Generally accepted formula: base salary × 1.25-1.40. Override DEV_HOUR_USD env (future) for org-specific rates.

aws_ec2_m5_xlarge_usd_per_hour.notes: 2× m5.large. Used as baseline for heavier-service estimation.

aws_hours_per_month.notes: Standard cloud-billing hours-per-month constant. AWS uses 730 for monthly cost projections.

hours_per_loc_added.citation: COCOMO II lower band; in-house 2026 baseline
hours_per_loc_added.notes: 3 minutes per LOC including review + tests. PR adding 100 LOC ≈ 5h of engineering time.

hours_per_file_touched.notes: DEPRECATED — new-feature dev-time is no longer modeled. The money axis now measures tech-debt servicing (bugs + maintenance + AI tokens), not the cost to write the feature. Kept for back-compat of the constants file.

bug_hours_critical.notes: Servicing hours for a Critical latent defect (correctness/security/runtime). Defect cost escalates ~10-100x from coding stage to production; a finding that ships is on the expensive end.

bug_hours_important.notes: Servicing hours for an Important finding.

bug_hours_minor.notes: Servicing hours for a Minor finding.

maint_hours_per_finding.notes: Ongoing iteration drag per debt item. Sonar: ~5,500 dev-hours/yr of remediation per 1M LOC; each debt item compounds maintenance time.

maint_hours_per_loc.notes: Annualized maintenance tax per LOC (Sonar ~0.0055 h/LOC/yr over a multi-year horizon). NOT the cost to write the LOC — that new-feature dev-time is intentionally not modeled.

llm_tokens_per_iteration.notes: Tokens an AI coding agent spends to make ONE change to this code, agentic baseline (~1M input + ~40K output incl. the re-read context snowball). Clean tasks are far cheaper (~33K); messy ones balloon (Cursor 188K w/ errors vs Claude 33K).

llm_expected_iterations.notes: Times this code is revisited by an AI agent over its maintenance life. Maintenance is ~60% of lifecycle cost; code is touched repeatedly.

llm_blended_usd_per_mtoken.notes: Blended agentic rate. At GPT-4.1 ($2/$8) / Claude-Sonnet ($3/$15) with a 25:1 input:output ratio the raw blend is ~$3.5/1M; the retry/self-correction snowball pushes the effective agentic cost to ~$5/1M.

cyclomatic_high_risk.description: Cyclomatic-complexity cutoff above which a function is flagged as 'complex' in the tech-debt block. Functions with complexity > N appear in pr_review_ext.tech_debt.high_complexity[].
cyclomatic_high_risk.notes: SonarQube + radon converge on >10 as the 'complex' cutoff.

long_function_loc.description: Function-LOC cutoff for the 'long function' smell. Functions with loc ≥ N appear in pr_review_ext.tech_debt.long_functions[].
long_function_loc.source_text: Robert C. Martin — Clean Code (functions should be < ~80 LOC)
long_function_loc.notes: Cutoff for the 'long function' smell. Wikipedia's Code smell page is the most stable URL covering this rule.

duplication_ratio_threshold.description: rapidfuzz-style ratio threshold (0..100). Function-name pairs at ≥ this ratio are clustered as duplicates.

duplication_max_compare_candidates.description: Hard cap on the pairwise comparison set to bound the O(n²) loop. Sorted-truncate above this; bounded Levenshtein early-exit keeps practical cost low.
duplication_max_compare_candidates.notes: Cap on pairwise comparison set. Bounded Levenshtein early-exit keeps practical cost much lower; 1500² is the worst case.

code_suggestions_confidence_threshold.description: Minimum drift-finding confidence (0..1) required to lift a finding into pr_review.code_suggestions[]. Per the spec's Quality Bar: 'Only emit a suggestion if confidence > threshold (default 0.75).'
code_suggestions_confidence_threshold.notes: Per-finding gate. Drift's ORM antipatterns are typically 0.85–0.95; lowering below 0.75 surfaces low-signal hits.

code_suggestions_before_lines_context.description: Number of context lines emitted above + below the flagged line in diff.before_lines for each suggestion. Higher = more LLM context; lower = smaller JSON.
code_suggestions_before_lines_context.notes: GitHub diff-hunks default to 3 lines of context. Symmetric: 3 before + 3 after + the flagged line = 7 lines total.

architecture_flow_max_roots_in_mermaid.description: Cap on number of affected-roots rendered into the after_mermaid flowchart. Above this we truncate to keep the diagram readable in a PR comment.
architecture_flow_max_roots_in_mermaid.notes: GitHub PR comments cap mermaid render at ~30 nodes; 8 roots × 6 children = 48 worst-case, but in practice rarely > 20.

architecture_flow_max_children_per_root.description: Cap on first-level callees rendered per affected root. Keeps the flowchart from exploding when one root has many callees.
architecture_flow_max_children_per_root.notes: Pairs with max_roots above for a worst-case bound of 48 nodes.

architecture_flow_max_data_structures.description: Cap on entries in the data_structures[] table. Top-N by method count; ties broken alphabetically.
architecture_flow_max_data_structures.citation: in-house — matches the spec's Image 1 table size
architecture_flow_max_data_structures.notes: The HTML mockup shows ~7 data structures; 12 leaves headroom without scrolling the table.

value_customer_features_normalizer.description: Denominator for the features-percentage term. delta% = min(features/N, 1) × 100. Setting N = 3 means '3 features = 100% on this term'.
value_customer_features_normalizer.citation: in-house heuristic — calibrated for typical PR sizes
value_customer_features_normalizer.notes: PRs adding ≥3 user-visible features clamp at the high end of the customer-value axis.

value_customer_features_weight.description: Linear weight applied to the features term in the customer-value axis. Combined with issues_weight; both sum to 1.0.
value_customer_features_weight.notes: Features weighted higher than issues+fixes (0.6 vs 0.4) because user-visible features are the primary 'customer value' signal.

value_customer_issues_weight.description: Linear weight applied to the (issues_resolved + bug_fixes) term in the customer-value axis.
value_customer_issues_weight.notes: Complementary to features_weight; the two MUST sum to 1.0.

value_runtime_perf_commit_weight.description: Percentage-points awarded per `perf:` Conventional-Commits prefix. Clamped at value_runtime_max_delta.
value_runtime_perf_commit_weight.notes: Without real benchmark data, a `perf:` commit is the strongest static signal. 15pp/commit caps a single perf-focused PR at the axis ceiling.

value_runtime_max_delta.description: Upper bound for value_runtime.delta_percent. Prevents runaway positive scores from many perf commits.
value_runtime_max_delta.notes: Matches the HTML mockup's runtime axis ceiling of +60%.

value_runtime_ux_new_test_weight.description: Percentage-points per new test file added. Higher than fixes/docs because tests durably improve dev-loop reliability.
value_runtime_ux_new_test_weight.notes: 5pp × 3 new test files = +15pp; cap at value_runtime_ux_max_delta.

value_runtime_ux_bug_fix_weight.description: Percentage-points per bug fix. Bug fixes improve dev experience (less debugging same bug twice).
value_runtime_ux_bug_fix_weight.notes: Lower than new tests (5pp) because a fix without a test can regress.

value_runtime_ux_docs_commit_weight.notes: Docs improve onboarding UX. Mid-range between fixes (3) and new tests (5).

value_runtime_ux_max_delta.notes: Matches the HTML mockup's runtime UX axis ceiling.

value_money_baseline_engineer_hours_per_.description: Hours-per-month one full-time engineer represents. Used as the denominator when normalizing PR cost/profit to a percent.

value_money_perf_profit_lookahead_months.description: Months over which `perf:` commit savings are projected (e.g. 1 m5.large worth of compute saved × 12 months = annual figure).
value_money_perf_profit_lookahead_months.notes: Annualizing makes the savings comparable to dev-hour costs (which are paid upfront).

value_money_cleanup_profit_factor.description: Fraction of the future-maintenance cost recovered when LOC is deleted vs added. delta% net deletions × hours_per_loc × 0.3 = cleanup_profit_hours.
value_money_cleanup_profit_factor.notes: Deletion saves future maintenance but doesn't recoup the full per-LOC cost — most of the cost was the writing, not the maintenance.

visual_summary_pr_size_likelihood_normal.description: Files-changed value at which the PR-size risk's likelihood saturates at 1.0. likelihood = min(files / N, 1).
visual_summary_pr_size_likelihood_normal.notes: Below 100 files, likelihood scales linearly. The spec calls out 100-file PRs as 'PR size · 100 files' in the risk-map example.

visual_summary_pr_size_severity_normaliz.description: Files-changed value at which PR-size severity term reaches 0.7 (max). severity = min(files/200 + 0.3, 1).
visual_summary_pr_size_severity_normaliz.notes: Severity floors at 0.3 (every PR has SOME blast-radius risk) and grows slowly — even a 200-file PR caps at 1.0.

visual_summary_blast_radius_min_roots.description: Affected-root count above which we emit a 'Wide blast radius' risk item.
visual_summary_blast_radius_min_roots.notes: Below 5 roots the blast radius is narrow enough not to warrant a dedicated risk pin.

visual_summary_untested_loc_threshold.description: LOC-added threshold above which 'untested code' risk fires WHEN no test files were touched.
visual_summary_untested_loc_threshold.notes: Below 200 LOC, missing test changes is usually acceptable (small refactor, doc fix). Above 200, the absence of tests is a meaningful signal.

tests_in_graph_uncovered_root_severity_n.description: Used by VS1's risks_from_ext_signals: severity = 0.55 + n_uncovered/20, clamped at 0.85. Likelihood normalizer = N.
tests_in_graph_uncovered_root_severity_n.notes: Uncovered roots are a stronger correctness signal than mere duplication; severity floors at 0.55.
